contest.title: 2024-2025 ICPC Asia Jakarta Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Read the corrected full statement, examples, and limits; shrine activation is immediate and mandatory on the first visit.
Organizer notice confirming that mandatory shrine activation is the intended semantics and that the statement was fixed after the contest.
Official Jakarta problem analysis used for the walking-segment normalization, three step types, R/S pivot DP, dominance lemma, and O(N log N) segment-tree sweep.
Actually read as a cross-check; it gives a materially different forward DP over the two post-flight stamina states and one lazy segment tree.
Reconciled both DP formulations and differentially tested the forward recurrence against exhaustive Dijkstra on the three official samples, the Luogu edge case, and 1000 random instances with N<=5.

annotation.summary: Move both the traveler and dragon from island 1 to island N on a line. A first human visit to island i immediately adds P_i stamina to the dragon, swimming spends D stamina, flying requires positive stamina and resets it to zero, and each directed walking tunnel may be used at most once; minimize total time.
annotation.constraints: 2 <= N <= 200000; 1 <= D, T_s, T_f, T_w, P_i <= 200000. Prefix stamina potentials and the answer require signed 64-bit integers. Quadratic transitions over pairs of islands are impossible under the 3-second limit.
annotation.core_idea: If T_f <= T_s, fly on every edge for answer (N-1)T_f: after each landing the mandatory shrine activation makes stamina positive again. Assume T_f > T_s. Normalize an optimum so the dragon only progresses right and each walking excursion is one forward interval immediately retraced; once-only directed tunnels make these intervals edge-disjoint, although consecutive intervals may touch. A step from x to y is then one of three forms: (1) walk to y-1 and back, then swim through to y; (2) walk to y-1 and back, swim to y-1, then fly to y, so shrine y activates on landing and the post-flight stamina is P_y; or (3) walk to y and back, swim to y-1, then fly to y, so shrine y was preactivated and the post-flight stamina is 0. Let Q_i=sum_{k<=i}P_k, R_i=Q_{i-1}-D(i-1), and S_i=Q_i-D(i-1). After a flight into x, all relevant history compresses to a pivot A: A=R_x after type 2 and A=S_x after type 3. A swim-ending step to y is feasible exactly when R_y>=A; a next flight ending at y has new pivot B=R_y or S_y and is feasible exactly when B>A-D. If B>=A, replacing that final flight by a swim is cheaper because T_f>T_s and preserves the no-larger old pivot A, so an optimal flight transition satisfies A-D<B<A. For fixed x,y and A, every intermediate island i with x<i<y and R_i>=A can be made a separate type-1 step, each saving 2T_w; with c such islands, transition cost to type e' is T_s(y-1-x)+T_f+2T_w(y-1-x-c+e')+dp[y][e']. Sort all 2N post-flight states by pivot and sweep A upward. Two segment trees indexed by destination y hold adjusted dp[y][e'] values for the active pivot window (A-D,A); query y>x, insert newly computed states, and expire B<=A-D. Whenever the sweep passes an R_i, island i stops being a free intermediate endpoint, so add 2T_w to every stored destination y>i with a suffix range update. Lazy range-add/range-min trees make every event logarithmic; equal pivots must be grouped to enforce B<A. Separately initialize the no-more-flight terminal choice, including the case of prewalking through island N when S_N>=A.
annotation.complexity: O(N log N) time and O(N) memory; the T_f <= T_s case is O(N) including input.
tricks: use the corrected mandatory-shrine semantics; the original optional wording defines a different problem
normalize once-only tunnel walks into non-overlapping out-and-back intervals
reduce every step to exactly three swim/fly endpoint types
compress arbitrary stamina history after a flight to one of two states and one prefix-sum pivot
discard nondecreasing-pivot flights by a dominance exchange
sweep the strict pivot window and turn lost intermediate endpoints into suffix cost updates
group equal pivot events and distinguish strict flight feasibility from non-strict swim feasibility
handle the terminal S_N previsit case separately
verification_notes: The current Codeforces statement says a shrine immediately activates on first visit, matching the organizer correction and the official analysis.
The two state conventions were reconciled: official type 2 leaves P_i and uses pivot R_i, while type 3 leaves 0 and uses pivot S_i; the Luogu forward formulation stores exactly t_i in {P_i,0}.
The official and forward formulations agree on the strict positive-stamina test for flying and the non-strict at-least-D test for swimming.

A direct O(N log N)-recurrence translation matched exhaustive shortest-path search over full position, stamina, visited-shrine, and directed-tunnel states on the three samples, the Luogu edge case, and 1000 random cases with N<=5.
main_agent_review_notes: All proposed tags already exist in the live taxonomy; no new tag or translation is needed.
Do not reuse analysis written for the original optional-shrine statement.

The official variant is source-confirmed. The alternative was source-read, algebraically reconciled, and differentially tested against exhaustive small-state search; no accepted submission source was inspected because Codeforces exposed only the verdict page.

solution_variants.name: official-pivot-sweep-range-add-min-dp
solution_variants.summary: Use the official backward DP on the two possible types of the most recent flight. Prefix potentials R_i and S_i reduce stamina feasibility to comparisons between scalar pivots. The dominance exchange restricts each next-flight transition to a strict width-D pivot window. Sweep states by pivot while two segment trees over destination index maintain adjusted future DP costs, suffix range additions for newly ineligible swim-step endpoints, suffix minima for y>x, insertion, and expiration; terminal all-swim cases are seeded separately.
solution_variants.complexity: O(N log N) time and O(N) memory.

solution_variants.name: forward-special-state-baseline-dp
solution_variants.summary: Maintain only post-flight states (j,t_j) with t_j in {0,P_j} and transformed key K_j=Q_j-t_j-jD. Start from K_1=-D and use the algebraic baseline (N-1)(2T_w+T_s). At island i, prefix-min queries over K_j create a type-3 state t_i=0 when K_j<=Q_i-D(i-1)-1 and a type-2 state t_i=P_i when K_j<=Q_{i-1}-D(i-1)-1, adding T_f-T_s. Insert them at keys Q_i-iD and Q_{i-1}-iD. States with K_j<=Q_{i-1}-iD can swim edge i-1 to i without the baseline round-trip walk, so a prefix range add of -2T_w rebates that cost. A final feasible prefix minimum plus the baseline yields the answer.
solution_variants.complexity: O(N log N) time and O(N) memory after coordinate-compressing the 2N keys.

tags.evidence: The main solution defines minimum remaining time for each island and last-flight type, then optimizes transitions to the next flight endpoint; the forward variant is an equivalent shortest-cost DP over post-flight states.
After any flight, the unbounded stamina and full visit history collapse to only two possibilities, post-flight stamina P_i or 0, equivalently the scalar pivots R_i or S_i.
For T_f>T_s, any flight whose new pivot B is at least the old pivot A is dominated by swimming the final edge, which is cheaper and leaves a no-worse smaller pivot; this removes all such transitions.
The O(N log N) optimization sorts all R_i/S_i pivot events and sweeps them, maintaining exactly the candidate DP states with A-D<B<A and updating walking penalties when each R_i threshold is crossed.
Two lazy segment trees support suffix additions of 2T_w to all destinations beyond a crossed island, range minima over y>x, point insertion, and expiration of states outside the pivot window.
DP states and threshold events are reordered by transformed stamina pivot rather than processed online in island order.
As A increases past R_i, island i's 2T_w saving disappears from every transition ending to its right, and this contribution change is maintained by one suffix update.
The forward variant creates each post-flight state from the minimum adjusted DP value among all earlier transformed keys K_j below a feasibility threshold.
Q_i and the derived R_i/S_i potentials turn every accumulated stamina feasibility condition over an interval into a constant-time scalar comparison.
